text: Traditional transition-based dependency parsers require manual feature engineering and typically have to make use of millions of sparse indicator features. These models tend to have a high feature extraction cost and suffer from poor generalization performance. In this paper, we propose a new neural network-based model for greedy transition-based dependency parsers. We embed words, POS tags and dependencies into low dimensional dense vector spaces and then learn a compact set of dense features using a novel cube activation function to model high-order interactions. The parser significantly outperforms previous models in terms of accuracy and speed. We report +2% (4% absolute) unlabeled and labeled attachment scores on English and Chinese. Our parser can parse more than 1,000 sentences per second while maintaining an unlabeled attachment score of 92.2% on the popular English Penn Treebank dataset.
headline: Fully Human Written
